Because they penetrate the visible surface of a painting, ultraviolet and other nonvisible spectra of light enable the detection of otherwise imperceptible features of the work. Beneath the surface of Claude Monet's *Wisteria* (1917–20), art historians found that Monet had initially included water lilies but ultimately painted over them.
